node.text:
For the seventh year in a row, we are thrilled to have been awarded Best Private Bank in the World by Global Finance Magazine, which annually recognizes the top institutions serving high-net-worth individuals and families. This award is a testament to our commitment to excellence and innovation on behalf of our clients, and we were honored to celebrate the achievements of our team at the recent Global Finance Magazine Awards dinner.

Awards or rankings are not indicative of future success or results. For third-party award selection criteria and other important information, tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
From everyone at J.P. Morgan Private Bank, we wish you a healthy and prosperous Year of the Horse.

In the lunar zodiac tradition, the Horse represents momentum, independence and the decisiveness to lead. We look forward to harnessing that spirit as we help clients move with purpose and perspective in the coming months. Happy Lunar New Year!

AI is shaking things up faster than most people expected, especially in the tech world. Last week, software company stocks took a big hit after new AI tools showed they could handle tasks like organizing data, tracking expenses and creating reports—things that used to require expensive software programs. Investors are worried that these traditional software companies may become obsolete.

Whether or not these concerns become reality, companies already using AI are seeing real benefits. Their margin expansion has outpaced that of companies that haven't yet adopted AI by over 3 percentage points. Disruption can have painful consequences, but the revenue opportunity and productivity gains will be worth it.

Tap the link in bio to know more.

As proud sponsors of the Sundance Film Festival, we were thrilled to bring together 200+ clients and guests for our final “Cocktails & Conversations” event in Park City, Utah. We heard an inspiring conversation about storytelling and the future of the film industry with one of Hollywood’s most versatile and dynamic talents, Olivia Wilde. 

The award-winning actor, director, producer and co-founder of investment firm Proximity Ventures recently celebrated her Sundance directorial debut with her latest film, “The Invite.” Thank you to Olivia and to all attendees for a truly memorable evening. 

To learn more about entertainment tap the link in bio.

node.text:
The past few weeks have been wild for gold. After surging to nearly $5,600/oz, gold experienced its steepest daily drop since 2013 on Friday at -8.9%, and its largest intraday decline in over a century at -14.0%. Despite this volatility, we believe gold will continue to benefit from investors and central banks seeking to marginally diversify how they store their wealth.

Zooming out, the yellow metal has long been the ultimate safe haven—a store of value that has endured for thousands of years. When markets feel uneasy, even with concerns about the U.S. dollar, investors often turn to gold. Historically, periods marked by such uncertainty in the U.S. dollar have coincided with surges in gold prices, reinforcing gold’s role as an effective diversifier.

node.text:
Unveiling the 2026 Global Family Office Report, a comprehensive look at how family offices worldwide are navigating new investment approaches and generational priorities. With insights from 333 family offices across 30 countries, discover how families are embracing AI, refining succession plans, and safeguarding portfolios against inflation and geopolitical risks. Explore the key findings and see how families are building resilience for the future. 

Tap the link in bio to download the full report.

#FamilyOfficeReport

node.text:
Performance isn’t just for athletes. As this episode’s guest David Howden proves, it is also essential in business. The energetic founder of the global insurance group Howden credits resilience and teamwork for his extraordinary success, believing that mistakes should be embraced as a sign of growth—not failure.

Our partnership with Performance People brings you candid insights from top leaders in sports and business. 

Tap the link in bio to watch the full podcast now.

node.text:
Founders spend years, decades and sometimes entire lifetimes creating companies. When the time comes to sell, we support them as they navigate the complexities of that transition, helping to shape a legacy that endures long after the transaction is complete.

“From evolution to exit” is our all-inclusive resource hub that empowers business leaders to shape their liquidity events with clarity and confidence. Within the campaign, our U.K. spotlight series draws on the perspectives of Private Bank leaders as they explore defining moments in this journey.

Join Marice Brown, UK Region Head, and James Chilvers, Head of UK Wealth Advisory, for a conversation on how the firm can best assist entrepreneurs in their ventures.

To discover more insights, tap the link in the bio.

node.text:
Chess takes center stage on the J.P. Morgan NextList 2026—a curated collection of books and experiences to spark big ideas and exploration. The legendary musical returns to the Imperial Theatre after 37 years, featuring ABBA’s iconic music and starring Aaron Tveit, Lea Michele and Nicholas Christopher. On opening night, JPM’s Darin Oduyoye joined the cast and guests on the red carpet to share why this bold revival is captivating audiences today.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We were proud to host our 18th Annual Women in Healthcare event in San Francisco in honor of the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference. Attendees gathered at the St. Regis hotel for a reception celebrating women executives and investment professionals, and heard from JPMorganChase leaders Jamie Dimon, Chairman and CEO, and Dorothee Blessing, Global Co-Head of Investment Banking Coverage, who shared their perspectives on the year ahead.

node.text:
Internationally renowned makeup artist and beauty founder Bobbi Brown sat down with BJ Goergen, Head of Morgan Private Advisory at the Private Bank, for a candid conversation on entrepreneurship, resilience and reinvention. Her new book, Still Bobbi, is spotlighted as a must-read on J.P. Morgan’s NextList2026, a collection of books and experiences curated to spark bold ideas and big thinking for the year ahead.
 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
In this year's Eye on the Market Outlook: Smothering Heights, Michael Cembalest, Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, highlights that most direct AI companies are showing strong free cash flow, with hyperscalers investing 35%-40% of revenue in capex and R&D, and Meta at nearly 70%. These firms are betting big on AI.

Tap the link in the bio to read the full Outlook.

The 2026 Eye on the Market Outlook: Smothering Heights.

In this year’s report, Michael Cembalest, Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, explores how a select group of hyperscalers and their semiconductor partners have reshaped the market. With 42 AI-driven companies powering most S&P 500 earnings and capital spending since ChatGPT’s launch, is this tech moat indestructible?

The Outlook also examines the risks ahead—from US power constraints to global supply chain vulnerabilities and the future of hyperscaler profits. 

For what’s next in the age of AI, tap the link in the bio to read the full report.

node.text: We wish you and your loved ones a holiday season filled with joy, peace and happiness. As we look back on the past year, we’re grateful for the relationships we’ve built and strengthened, and for the opportunity to support you and your family along the way. We look forward to what the new year will bring. Warmest wishes from all of us at J.P. Morgan Private Bank.

node.text: The 2025 Global Private Bank Digital Innovation Competition sparked creativity and collaboration worldwide, as Analysts and Associates pitched bold, AI-driven solutions to transform client and advisor journeys. After an exciting round of finals, Team Tax Compass with members Helena Wolk, Serena DiBianco, and Lizzie Dunigan, emerged as the winners and will continue to partner with technology to bring their idea to life. Kudos to every participant for pushing boundaries and making a lasting impact.

node.text:
Sir Ben Ainslie, Chief Executive Officer of Emirates GBR, recently sat down for a very special edition of the Performance People podcast. In a wide-ranging and honest conversation with Georgie Ainslie, Sir Ben spoke candidly about the challenges and rewards of managing high-level sports teams and tournaments.

With all eyes on the 2027 America’s Cup—the world’s oldest international sports competition—Sir Ben explained how important finding the right partnerships has been in planning its continued success. With the tournament at a crossroads in its history, he hopes the recent success of SailGP will offer renewed and enthusiastic interest as the Cup heads to Naples in just over a year—and ensure that its longstanding legacy continues.

Tap the link in the bio to discover the full podcast.

node.text:
A remarkable season. A resilient team. An incredible partnership—and we’re proud to continue to stand with Emirates GBR into 2026. 

Tap the link in the bio to discover more.

node.text:
We wrapped up an incredible year of our global Emerging Family Leaders events with a truly memorable gathering in Bangkok—bringing the series to individuals in Asia Pacific for the very first time. 

The energy, ideas and connections made at this milestone occasion set the stage for the next generation of leaders and innovators to thrive and shape the future of enterprise across the region.

Tap the link in the bio to see the highlights and explore the moments that made this gathering unforgettable.

Around the world, Endeavor is dedicated to fostering a culture where entrepreneurs support one another, amplify their impact and contribute to long-term economic growth. For over a decade, we have partnered with this global entrepreneurial community, bringing together founders, mentors and investors to exchange ideas, forge new connections and inspire the next generation. This year, we celebrated high-impact entrepreneurship around the globe, and we look forward to building on this momentum in 2026. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Innovation Economy Group dedicated to empowering entrepreneurs and founders.

The birthFUND 100 Legacy Dinner brought together cultural leaders, advocates and changemakers for an evening of insightful dialogue on the power of generational wealth to drive positive change. Our Women and Wealth Strategy Team was honored to sponsor this inspiring gathering in support of birthFUND’s mission to expand access to quality, life-saving maternal care. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Diverse Wealth Strategies events provide a space for connection, education and community engagement. They are open to all who are interested in participating, regardless of background.

We were thrilled to sponsor the TiE Singapore Annual Gala Dinner—an inspiring evening that celebrated innovation, fostered meaningful connections and spotlighted our “Pathway to Exit” initiative. Developed in partnership with TiE, this program empowers mid- to late-stage startups to achieve successful exits.

Paul Thompson, Head of Singapore and Southeast Asia at J.P. Morgan Private Bank, joined Sridhar Pinnapureddy, serial entrepreneur and CEO of CtrlS Datacenters & Cloud4C Services, for a panel discussion titled “Bootstrapped to Profitable Exit.” Together, they shared actionable insights with the entrepreneurial community.

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how we serve tech entrepreneurs, founders and disruptors.

There are a lot more people trying to sell homes than buy them in the U.S. housing market. You’d expect prices to be falling, but instead they’re up 60% nationwide since 2019 and still rising by nearly 4% a year.

Why? It’s mostly about affordability and a lack of supply. High mortgage rates and expensive home prices mean the monthly payment for a typical home is now more than double what it was before the pandemic. That makes it tough for first-time buyers to get into the market. For those who already own, about half have locked in mortgage rates below 4%, so they have little reason to sell and take on a bigger payment.

At the same time, years of not building enough homes has left the country with a shortage of nearly 3 million units—a gap that could take a decade to close. Although rates might come down a bit for shorter-term loans, they’re expected to stay high for longer-term borrowing—so mortgage rates probably won’t drop much overall. The good news is that renting remains much more affordable, with monthly rent more than 30% cheaper than monthly payments on a home.

Your world moves fast and deserves support that keeps pace. Through Lifestyle Services, our Family Office Practice connects U.S. Private Bank clients to a curated network of premier providers. From private aviation and luxury travel to staffing and healthcare management, every connection is curated, and every detail is handled, so you can focus on what truly matters. 

Tap the link in bio to explore Lifestyle Services.

J.P. Morgan Private Bank was honored to serve as the official sponsor of the 2025 Emerging Talent Spotlight with the Latin GRAMMY Foundation, held alongside the Latin Recording Academy Person of the Year Gala™ and the 26th Annual Latin GRAMMY Awards®. This celebration brought together some of the most influential voices in music to celebrate the arts, creativity and culture.

J.P. Morgan is proud to partner with organizations that nurture the next generation of creative talent and leadership. This sponsorship underscores the Private Bank’s commitment to supporting world-class platforms that inspire new ideas and connect people across borders.

Tap the link in bio and learn more about our holistic approach to building wealth that makes an impact.

Explore the factors behind the “full pool” and what the stall in distributions means for investors in today’s alternatives landscape.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
The American economy has held up better than expected, despite a challenging year marked by rising tariffs, geopolitical tensions and the longest government shutdown in U.S. history. Companies used healthy profit margins to manage higher costs, consumer spending held up, and while the labor market softened, layoffs remained limited. The point: The economy has weathered multiple shocks and is still on its feet.

As the Federal Reserve continues to cut rates, the outlook is brightening. During the spring’s trade uncertainty, economists predicted GDP would grow just 1.4% in 2025, down from 2.8% in 2024. Since then, forecasts have steadily improved, with the consensus now at 1.9%. Meanwhile, holiday spending is on track to set a new record—another piece of evidence that the economy has bent but not broken.

node.text:
AI boom or bubble? Sitara Sundar, Head of Alternative Investment Strategy & Market Intelligence, explores this question with a four-part framework in our 2026 Outlook Deep Dive. #Outlook2026 #jpmorganprivatebank 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Guided by the principle that talent is universal while opportunity is not, we are proud to support John Wood, Founder of U-GO, and their mission to help women in historically underserved communities pursue higher education.
 
Harshika Patel, CEO of J.P. Morgan Private Bank Asia, and Pia Cooke, Head of Australia for J.P. Morgan Private Bank, sponsored the U-GO fundraising galas in Hong Kong and Sydney, bringing together philanthropists, change-makers and the firm’s dedicated analyst and associate volunteers to help raise funds for educational scholarships for students across Asia. 
 
Discover how J.P. Morgan Private Bank can help you achieve your philanthropic goals to create a lasting legacy by tapping the link in bio.

During the internet revolution that began in the late 1990s, most value in the early stages of building physical and digital infrastructure went to public companies. Later, as platforms and applications emerged, private companies captured a larger share of the value.

The AI boom has followed a similar pattern so far, with public companies benefiting from infrastructure development. But looking ahead, private markets are likely to play an even bigger role. In the past, the typical tech company went public at eight years old with $44 million in revenue. Today, the median tech IPO happens at 14 years and $220 million in revenue—meaning companies are creating much more value before reaching public markets.

As a result, many promising AI innovations—such as agentic AI systems and industry-specific applications—may stay private longer, limiting early investment opportunities for public market investors. 

#Outlook2026 #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
Paris set the stage for a premier gathering of rising leaders, where some of the world’s best emerging business minds came together for two unforgettable days of inspiration and discovery. 

Guests connected with trailblazers in technology, wellness and leadership, enjoyed exclusive access to Notre-Dame, dined at Collège des Bernardins and soaked up the city’s vibrant culture.

We continue to be energized by the connections made and the ideas shared by the next generation, and look forward to supporting them as they shape the future.

node.text:
What are the next big ideas that will redefine our world? This year’s J.P. Morgan NextList spotlights a new collection of books and experiences at the forefront of arts, culture, innovation and beyond—each chosen to spark creative thinking and bold exploration for the year ahead. #Nextlist2026

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We were back in vibrant Miami for our Emerging Family Leaders global series, where an incredible day of learning, networking and inspiration took center stage. Against the backdrop of Miami’s energy, attendees explored cutting-edge AI insights and heard dynamic entrepreneurship stories through panel discussions that broke barriers and sparked innovation. 

Tap the link in bio to dive into the excitement and learn more about the series.

Sometimes the investing landscape is hard to read. In prior year Outlooks, we often struggled with tangled debates and ambiguous data. The picture looked cloudy and blurred.

Not this year. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

#Outlook2026 #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
Our 2026 Outlook: Promise and Pressure examines the major forces set to shape 2026 and beyond—artificial intelligence, global fragmentation and inflation. Read our annual Outlook to see what they could mean for your investment strategy. #Outlook2026 #jpmorganprivatebank 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Our Asian Wealth Strategies team welcomed clients, colleagues and friends to Diwali celebrations across the country, inviting everyone to share in the joy and reflect on the meaning of the Festival of Lights: the triumph of light over darkness and the promise of renewal. With lively performances and festive flavors, the gatherings honored both tradition and togetherness, reminding us of the cultural ties that strengthen our communities. 

Learn more about our Asian Wealth Strategies team and our holistic approach to helping our clients achieve their financial goals by tapping the link in bio.

Asian Wealth Strategies events provide a space for connection, education and community engagement. They are open to all who are interested in participating, regardless of background.

Ahead of the public opening of Paris Photo 2025, we invited distinguished guests to an exclusive pre-vernissage, where they enjoyed an early preview of the exciting work featured in this year’s exhibition. 

Bringing together 179 galleries and 43 publishers from 33 countries, this annual art fair has grown into an international celebration of photography—championing established and emerging creative talent from across the world.

In our recent article, we spoke to fair director Florence Bourgeois about the event’s growing popularity, its French heritage and how new technologies are creating opportunities for artists and collectors alike.

Tap the link in bio and find out about the shared values behind our partnership.

We recently held our sixth annual Asia Portfolio Perspectives event in Hong Kong and Singapore, bringing together clients and industry professionals for engaging discussions. Senior leaders, including Harshika Patel, Lan You and Albert Yang from J.P. Morgan Private Bank Asia, as well as Hamilton Reiner, Ian Fenwick, Anton Pil, Anya Schiess, Patrick McGoldrick, Joseph Wilson and Brian Coleman from J.P. Morgan Asset Management, shared key opportunities in credit, equities and active ETFs—areas that saw strong inflows in 2025.

Attendees gained valuable perspectives on market trends and strategies, underscoring our commitment to innovative, diversified portfolio solutions and our leadership in guiding clients through the evolving financial landscape.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.
This #VeteransDay, we pay tribute to the brave individuals who have served our country with unwavering dedication. Join us in honoring their courage and commitment.

We were honored to celebrate 90 years of partnership and history in Saudi Arabia.
 
JPMorganChase Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon joined colleagues, clients and valued partners to commemorate nearly a century of deep-rooted relationships in the region. He shared his forward-looking vision for unlocking new opportunities in the country, and reaffirmed our unwavering commitment to its growth and prosperity.
 
Tap the link in bio and discover more about our commitment to building strong relationships with the clients, partners and communities we serve.

node.text:
Sometimes the investing landscape in the year ahead is hard to read. Not this year. In the 2026 Private Bank Outlook, we identify three key drivers reshaping the world now and in the foreseeable future. We also offer a range of concrete steps you might consider taking as a result. Discover all this and more when the Outlook launches on November 17.

#Outlook2026 #jpmorganprivatebank

We were honored to be named Best Global Private Bank for the second consecutive year and Best Private Bank for Alternatives at the 2025 Global Private Banking Awards, hosted by PWM/The Banker. These prestigious awards celebrate outstanding achievement in private banking worldwide, with winners chosen through comprehensive qualitative and quantitative evaluations by a panel of 23 industry experts. This recognition reflects our ongoing commitment to delivering exceptional service and innovative solutions for our clients. Thank you to our clients and teams for making this achievement possible.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Please note that awards or rankings are not indicative of future success or results. For third-party award selection criteria and other important information, tap the link in bio.

node.text:
What’s driving change in the entertainment industry, and what does it mean for legacy media companies? In his latest Eye on the Market, Michael Cembalest, Asset and Wealth Management’s Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, unpacks the latest developments and what they could mean for the future of content creation.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

It’s now day 36 of the U.S. government shutdown—the longest in history.

While headlines have been focused on political gridlock, the impact has mainly been felt in missing economic data and growing pressure on millions of Americans. With key agencies such as the Departments of Commerce and Labor furloughed, investors are left relying on alternative sources to gauge the economy’s health.

The economic impact is starting to be felt. Funding for SNAP food assistance ran out this weekend, curtailing a program that aids about 40 million people. If the shutdown drags on for a full month with SNAP halted, GDP could shrink by 1.2 percentage points; a full quarter would mean a recession-sized hit of more than 3.5 percentage points. So far, markets have stayed calm, but the longer the shutdown drags on, the more the economic costs—and the urgency to resolve it—will grow.

node.text:
We’re excited to present Principal Discussions 2025: Conversations with the world’s wealthiest families. 

Through candid conversations with 111 billionaire principals, across 28 countries and over 15 industries, the study provides a rare glimpse into how families are stewarding their wealth, businesses, communities and future generations. 

We hope you find the report both insightful and inspiring. This is their wealth, in their words.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
To celebrate the fifth edition of our Top 200 Women-Powered Businesses Report, we recently hosted Behind the Brand—an exclusive evening of discussions and networking at Wild by Tart, London.

Business leaders, investors and clients came together to hear from Colette Laxton, Co-Founder of The INKEY List; Vivien Wong, Founder of Little Moons; and Victoria van Lennep, Co-Founder of Lendable, who shared their experiences and insights about building their brands and innovating and scaling for growth. 
 
In the half decade since our first report, we’ve seen a rapid rise in the number of U.K. women-powered businesses. Discover the businesses fuelling growth and driving innovation. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

JPMorganChase hosted an extraordinary evening of reflection and leadership at the newly opened National Medal of Honor Museum in Arlington, Texas.

The event brought together two recipients of the Medal of Honor, our nation’s highest award for valor in combat—Sal Giunta and Clint Romesha—for a moving conversation on courage, humility and what it truly means to lead in service to others. Moderated by Linda Bammann, member of the JPMorganChase & Co. Board of Directors and liaison to the firm’s Military & Veteran Affairs, the discussion reminded us that leadership is not about recognition, but responsibility—to your team, your mission and your community.

The National Medal of Honor Museum stands as a living tribute to the values of courage, sacrifice and character. Set within its immersive exhibits honoring acts of valor and the human stories behind them, the evening invited guests to reflect on how these same principles define true leadership. 

At JPMorganChase, we share that belief, aiming to lead with purpose, serve with humility, and help our clients, colleagues and communities build lasting legacies.

This event also marked an important milestone in the firm’s ongoing commitment to military families. As part of that mission, J.P. Morgan Private Bank has launched a new Military & Veteran Wealth Strategy, a dedicated effort to help service members, veterans and their families build, preserve and transition wealth with the same discipline and integrity that define their service. 

To explore the Private Bank’s new initiative, tap the link in bio. 

Diverse Wealth Strategies events provide a space for connection, education and community engagement. They are open to all who are interested in participating, regardless of background.

We’re proud to support the “A Night to Empower Change” gala in Hong Kong, celebrating 10 years of the Asian University for Women Hong Kong Support Foundation. The event united inspiring leaders, alumnae and friends to champion scholarships for future women changemakers.
 
Harshika Patel, CEO of J.P. Morgan Private Bank, Asia, highlighted our dedication to advancing education and leadership opportunities for all, including women. The evening fostered meaningful connections and opened doors for future.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
It was an incredible two days at the fourth annual Asian American Summit. Over 160 families and changemakers joined us in Northern California to explore the future of wealth management, investment strategies and the impact of AI across industries.

J.P. Morgan leaders and top Asian American innovators led dynamic panels and masterclass workshops, offering actionable insights on entrepreneurship, philanthropy and building generational legacies.

Thank you to our speakers, partners and attendees for making this event unforgettable. Together, we’re shaping a future of growth, leadership and lasting impact. #AsianWealthStrategies #AsianAmericanSummit

Tap the link in bio to learn more. 

Diverse Wealth Strategies events provide a space for connection, education and community engagement. They are open to all who are interested in participating, regardless of background.

Now in their 30th year, J.P. Morgan Asset Management’s Long-Term Capital Market Assumptions (LTCMAs) remain central to how we build portfolios for the next decade and beyond. These assumptions address one of the biggest issues on investors’ minds right now: high equity valuations. While elevated valuations are expected to be a headwind to equity returns, they are more than outweighed by the tailwinds of strong revenue growth, steady buybacks and reliable dividends.

Take the U.S. large-cap market as an example: While high valuations subtract 2% from expected returns, revenues add 6%, buybacks contribute 3%, and dividends add another 1.7%. Even after accounting for margin pressure and dilution, the net result is a projected total return of 6.7% for USD-based investors. That’s why, despite the S&P 500 more than doubling since October 2022, we still see room for healthy returns ahead when all the pieces are assembled.

We were honored to partner with Robin Hood for the 13th year at the J.P. Morgan / Robin Hood Investors Conference, which has raised over $65 million to help fight poverty in New York City. The conference hosted wide-ranging conversations on market outlooks, big-picture ideas from Michael Cembalest’s Eye on the Market, private credit, artificial intelligence, healthcare and stablecoins. We also heard best-idea pitches from leading managers and fireside chats with industry leaders such as Jamie Dimon, Dario Amodei, Kristin Kallergis Rowland and Ken Griffin.

J.P. Morgan Private Bank was proud to participate in the 80th Annual Alfred E. Smith Memorial Foundation Dinner at the Waldorf Astoria New York. The event featured inspiring keynotes from former Secretaries of State Antony J. Blinken and Michael R. Pompeo. Congratulations to Robert K. Kraft, recipient of the 2025 Happy Warrior Award. Together, we helped raise $7.3 million, bringing the five-year total to over $35 million in support of women and children in need.

The film industry drives innovation and creativity, provides millions of jobs, supports small businesses and enriches communities worldwide. By fostering new technologies and opportunities both on and off screen, it remains a valued global export and a cornerstone of cultural progress.

We are proud to support the Academy Museum of Motion Pictures, the world’s largest museum dedicated to global cinema and the arts, sciences and artists of moviemaking. Our own Olivier de Givenchy, Chairman of the Board of Trustees, and Malak Santini, Managing Director, represented the firm at the fifth annual gala, which raised essential funds for exhibitions, screenings, and educational programs.

As an official partner, we support the museum’s mission to advance and celebrate cinema through inclusive exhibitions and programs that inspire connection, joy and discovery for communities worldwide. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Credit: Getty Images for Academy Museum of Motion Pictures, Hunter Abrams / ©Academy Museum Foundation, Roger Kisby / ©Academy Museum Foundation

Dr. David Kelly, David Lebovitz, Stephen Parker and Gabriela Santos joined today's Monday Morning Meeting to discuss the launch of the 30th Anniversary edition of J.P. Morgan Asset Management’s 2026 Long-Term Capital Market Assumptions. Explore the time-tested projections that have helped clients build stronger portfolios for the past 30 years at the link in our bio.

Last week, leaders from across our global business gathered at our new worldwide headquarters in Midtown Manhattan for our Global Senior Leaders Conference. We focused on shaping the future of our organization—exploring new opportunities, driving innovation and strengthening global collaboration. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how the Private Bank can help you.

node.text:
Follow us as we look back on an extraordinary few days spent in the U.S. capital. 

Our Emerging Family Leaders event was filled with fascinating insights and conversations. In the heart of Washington, D.C., we harnessed perspectives from Georgetown University professors, explored the historic campus, and engaged in a series of stimulating panels at the iconic J.P. Morgan Bowen Building before enjoying an evening boat tour of the city.

Tap the link in bio to learn more about the series.

This week, the Law Firm and Professional Services Group at J.P. Morgan Private Bank had the privilege of hosting an exclusive gathering with Jamie Dimon, JPMorganChase Chairman and Chief Executive Officer. The event brought together leading attorneys and professionals for an evening of insightful conversation, connection and collaboration. The discussion was moderated by Bola Oyesanya, Head of the Law Firm and Professional Services Group, and hosted by Stacey Friedman, Executive Vice President and General Counsel. Together, we explored topics that matter most to our legal community. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how our Law Firm and Professional Services Group can help you.

The six months since “Liberation Day” have shown how quickly markets can rebound from shocks and uncertainty. Despite trade disruptions and political gridlock, companies and markets have adapted, driving a strong rally. That resilience has powered an extraordinary run—the current bull market is close to doubling in value after just three years, far faster than usual. Historically, bull markets take five years or more to double, but this rally has been unusually strong and steady. The key takeaway: Investors who stayed focused on long-term growth, rather than daily headlines, have been rewarded.

We were honored to sponsor the Visionary Women Summit 2025, which celebrated a decade of impact, resilience and community with Visionary Women. It was inspiring to attend this special event and see J.P. Morgan Private Bank's  Malak Santini present the 2025 Changemaker Award to Alex Morgan—Olympic Gold Medalist and World Cup Champion, Co-captain of the U.S. Women’s National Team. It was an incredible day filled with uplifting stories, real change, and a shared commitment to empowering women to lead and thrive. #2025changemaker

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
Empowering people is at the heart of Katie Brody’s mission in Pasadena. As Market Team Lead, Katie is dedicated to helping her team bring their best to the clients they serve across California. Whether you’re focused on building, preserving or transferring your wealth, our dedicated specialists are here to help you achieve your goals. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Pasadena team.

Our 13th annual Emerging Family Leaders event in the windy city of Chicago was an exclusive opportunity for our attendees to hear from athletes and entrepreneurs alike, who imparted personal insights of their successes and obstacles faced in the process. #JPMorganPBEFL #PBEmergingFamilyLeaders

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how J.P. Morgan Private Bank can help you navigate your wealth creation journey.

Beyond just powering U.S. stocks, artificial intelligence is making waves in the real economy, too. Tech spending has become a major engine of U.S. growth, with software and IT investments accounting for half of recent GDP gains. No surprise there: the Magnificent 7 alone are set to spend nearly $500 billion this year—about twice the government’s R&D budget. Tech+ now makes up around 50% of the S&P 500 and is behind 60% of the market’s gains so far this year. And this momentum doesn’t look set to slow. With only 10% of companies currently using AI, according to the Census Bureau, there’s still a long runway ahead, making tech a powerful force for both markets and the broader economy.

Happy Mid-Autumn Festival! 

Harshika Patel shares her warm wishes for the season. To celebrate, our colleagues came together for a festive lantern-making workshop.

node.text:
The U.S. government entered a shutdown last night, as lawmakers failed to pass a new funding bill before the deadline. “Nonessential” agencies like the EPA and Labor Department are closed, but “essential” services—national security, public safety and medical care—remain open.

What does this mean for markets? Investors have historically treated shutdowns as non-events, so there hasn't been a clear market impact—on average, the S&P 500 has continued its typical positive trend in the days before or after a shutdown. Importantly, a government shutdown has no bearing on Treasury debt or principal repayment at maturity. While a prolonged shutdown can briefly weigh on economic activity (each week of a shutdown is typically estimated to reduce quarterly GDP growth by about 0.1–0.2 percentage points, with most losses recouped once the government reopens), we think the shutdown remains low on the list of market drivers right now.
In honor of #HispanicHeritageMonth, we celebrate the vibrant cultures, unique stories and significant contributions of our Hispanic and Latino colleagues throughout the firm.

node.text:
In his latest Eye on the Market, Michael Cembalest, our Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, examines the extraordinary concentration of market gains among a select group of AI-related stocks. Explore the numbers behind this trend, with 40 companies now responsible for the vast majority of returns, earnings growth and capital spending since late 2022.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The ProXimo conference in San Diego brought together leaders and innovators from business, real estate, tech, sports, media and finance to celebrate the brilliance and impact of community, inspiring attendees to build their legacies, drive progress, and advance professional and economic mobility. 

As part of the conference, our Hispanic and Latino Wealth Strategies Team sponsored the Masters and Genius session and VIP dinner, which featured discussions with honorees including Rhodes Scholars John Philip Santos and Juan Sepúlveda; award-winning poet Richard Blanco; MacArthur Fellows Cristina Jiménez, Sandra Cisneros and Ruth Behar; and Olympic champions Marco Fabián and Luis Scola.

Guests gained firsthand insights from leaders shaping communities and industries, connected with peers, and celebrated the impact of Latino professionals and innovators. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how we can work together to explore the full possibility of your wealth.

Hispanic and Latino Wealth Strategies events provide a space for connection, education and community. They are open to all who are interested in participating, regardless of background.

The Federal Reserve cut rates last week for the first time since December 2024. In their projections, most officials expected one to two more cuts this year. The Fed sees firmer growth, slightly lower unemployment and a touch more inflation in the future.
 
Lower interest rates typically mean reduced returns on U.S. cash and bonds, and a less attractive U.S. dollar for global investors. What does this mean for portfolios? The classic Fed-cutting playbook is in motion. In this week’s Top Market Takeaways, we focus on diversifying internationally. While we remain constructive on U.S. equities, European and EM stocks are trading at a significant discount to the United States (about 15x forward earnings versus 22x for the S&P 500). Adding in a potentially softer dollar, these factors make international equities appealing as a means to balance risk and potentially improve long-term returns.

In our inaugural partnership with the Asian University Basketball League (AUBL), we hosted clients at an exclusive luncheon and the AUBL 2025 Championship Game in Hangzhou, China. This exciting event offered our guests a unique opportunity to enjoy the tournament and network with like-minded individuals, athletes and industry leaders.

Under the theme “Leadership in Sports,” Harshika Patel, CEO of J.P. Morgan Private Bank for Asia, highlighted our dedication to supporting clients’ sports passions. Joe Tsai, Chairman of Alibaba Group, and Yao Ming, Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Famer, joined Jintian Jay Li, CEO of the AUBL, for a compelling conversation about their journeys and insights on the sports industry. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
In Charlotte, our culture defines how we work together and with our clients. With a shared commitment to excellence, our advisors, bankers and specialists support executives, entrepreneurs and multigenerational families across the Carolinas in achieving their unique goals. 

To learn more about our Charlotte team tap the link in bio.

node.text:
Follow us as we kick off our Autumn Emerging Family Leaders series at the iconic Tower of London. 

Bringing guests together from across Europe, the Middle East and Africa, this memorable two-day event included a private tour of the Crown Jewels and an exclusive dinner.

The following day, attendees immersed themselves in thought-provoking sessions and collaborative discussions, forging new connections and exchanging fresh ideas. 

Tap the link in the bio to find out more.

J.P. Morgan Private Bank in Chicago recently hosted “Ignite, Inspire, Innovate!”—an event celebrating leadership and innovation. We welcomed Angie Long, co-founder and co-owner of the Kansas City Current and CIO of Palmer Square Capital Management; Giuliana Rancic, entertainment journalist, bestselling author, fashion designer and television personality; Karen Murphy, COO of the Chicago Bears; Laura Ricketts, Executive Chairperson of the Chicago Stars and board member of the Chicago Cubs; and Mellody Hobson, Co-CEO and President of Ariel Investments.

Changemakers in their respective fields, they each shared perspectives on inspiring progress, fostering collaboration and strengthening community engagement. 

Tap the link in bio to discover how J.P. Morgan Private Bank in Chicago can help support your goals.

node.text: The Federal Reserve is expected to cut rates today, but some investors are worrying that long-term yields will remain sticky, and they’re questioning if this will actually ease financial conditions. It’s important to remember that the 10-year yield is just one part of the story. Economists use “financial conditions indices,” which blend rates across the Treasury curve, borrowing costs, stocks, dollar strength and market volatility for a more complete picture. In the Fed’s own index, negative readings signal a tailwind for growth. Right now, the index points to a modest but meaningful 0.5% boost to the GDP over the next year despite the sticky 10-year yield.

node.text:
J.P. Morgan Private Bank is proud to unveil its fifth annual Top 200 Women-Powered U.K. Businesses Report, produced in collaboration with Beauhurst. 

Since its first release, this study has delivered invaluable insights—spotlighting the trends, opportunities and barriers women may face in realizing their entrepreneurial ambitions. This year, as we take stock of extraordinary progress—from creating jobs to shaping industries—the innovation of women-powered enterprises continues to have an undeniable impact on the United Kingdom’s high-growth ecosystem.

Join us as we celebrate their achievements.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
Since 1909, we have steadily grown our presence in the Middle East and Turkey, and now serve clients throughout this evolving and vibrant region. 

To celebrate over 100 years of service and collaboration, we’ve traced the origins of this proud journey. In doing so, we reflect on defining moments and enduring relationships, and reaffirm our commitment to the area’s continued prosperity. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management proudly hosted the first event in JPMorganChase Tower’s Exchange Hall. During its Monday Morning Meeting, they explored the latest market insights, policy impacts, advisor perspectives, and more.

The Federal Reserve is about to resume its rate-cutting cycle—a key theme in our latest Top Market Takeaways. So what happens when rates are cut? Cash yields drop, but the yield curve usually prices this in ahead of time. Markets are forward-looking, so money market yields are typically the last to adjust. That’s why high-quality corporate bonds stand out right now, offering yields well above those of government bonds and cash. Investment grade bonds with maturities under 10 years provide a meaningful yield advantage across the curve. While spreads are tight, we believe corporate bonds offer a compelling way to lock in higher income and add stability as the rate environment shifts.

Under sun-filled skies, we hosted a clinic at the iconic West Side Tennis Club in Forest Hills, New York for tennis enthusiasts. We had the honor of welcoming Tracy Austin, James Blake, Bob and Mike Bryan, and Lindsay Davenport, who shared their expertise through drills, instruction and stories from their careers. We enjoyed watching the final US Open matches after learning from these legends of the game. #jpmorganprivatebank #usopen

The US Open is the ultimate showcase of talent and determination, making it a must-watch for tennis fans everywhere. David Frame, CEO of J.P. Morgan’s Global Private Bank, was proud to present the winner’s check to the men’s champion and shared, “Being able to witness such excellence and skill both on and off the court is as impressive as it is exhilarating. Congratulations to all of this year’s players for their hard work, grit and inspiration.” #usopen #jpmorganprivatebank

The S&P 500’s expected earnings have been steadily climbing from 2024 into 2025. As we move through the second quarter, earnings are now projected to rise by about 12%, up from the 4.9% predicted in June—a testament to resilient demand and tighter cost control. This boost is mainly driven by strong performances in sectors such as tech, communication and finance, which together make up over half of the index’s profits. Despite economic challenges, 81% of S&P 500 companies have exceeded earnings expectations. As we transition into the third quarter, the focus will shift to trade policy and the Federal Reserve, but market sentiment remains positive overall.

We kicked off the first night of the US Open with an incredible conversation with Billie Jean King, 39-time Grand Slam title holder, tennis legend, sports icon and equality champion. Stacey Friedman, General Counsel for JPMorganChase, delivered opening remarks at the event hosted by J.P. Morgan in partnership with the National Women’s Law Center and its President and CEO Fatima Goss Graves. The arena was abuzz with excitement as tennis enthusiasts gathered to celebrate the athleticism and passion for the sport. #usopen #jpmorganprivatebank

From thought-provoking conversations to meaningful connections, it was a powerful week on Martha’s Vineyard with our Black Wealth Strategies Team. As proud sponsors of the SALA Series, we joined an inspiring community of executives, leaders and changemakers to exchange ideas and explore ways to deepen impact.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Black Wealth Strategies events provide a space for connection, education and community engagement. They are open to all who are interested in participating—regardless of background.

Bonds have long served as a buffer against stock market downturns, especially during growth scares. From the late 1990s to the 2020s, U.S. Treasuries often rose when the S&P 500 fell, providing a hedge against equity losses. However, post-pandemic inflation concerns have led to higher interest rates and altered stock-bond correlations. Since 2022, bonds have become more volatile and correlated with stocks, reducing their hedging effectiveness. Factors such as deglobalization, U.S. fiscal deficits and labor supply issues increase the likelihood of prolonged inflation above target—which raises the risk of simultaneous negative returns for bonds and equities. Therefore, it’s crucial to position for a regime where inflation is a concern and stock-bond correlation can be positive. Consider adding exposures that diversify equities and perform well when inflation is firm.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We are delighted to share our co-hosted event with Bvlgari, “An Evening of Timeless Elegance: Where Luxury Meets Legacy.” Guests were treated to an exclusive showcase of the latest High Jewellery Collection, flown in from Rome for its Australian debut, and had the opportunity to engage in conversations on the intersection of timeless craftsmanship and enduring heritage.
 
At J.P. Morgan Private Bank, we are committed to helping clients preserve and grow their wealth for a lasting legacy.

node.text:
During the home race in Portsmouth, Sir Ben Ainslie, Chief Executive Officer of Emirates Great Britain SailGP Team, spoke to us for a special edition of the Performance People podcast. He discussed how, among the dramatic scenes of the 2013 America’s Cup, the initial concept of SailGP took shape.

Hosted by Georgie Ainslie, the conversation also featured tournament co-founder Sir Russell Coutts and Team Italy co-owner and co-founder Jimmy Spithill. Together with Sir Ben, the sailing greats offered insights on the power of perseverance, the demands of leadership and the rapid rise of the event itself. 

Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

This month, the NBA approved the record-breaking sale of the Boston Celtics. @jpmorgan, collaborating with teams, owners and leagues, has financed 10 of the last 15 major sports transactions, helping clients achieve historic milestones in the sports industry.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

NBA is not affiliated with JPMorganChase or any of its affiliates or subsidiaries.

node.text: Corporates are looking past tariff challenges and focusing on long-term fundamentals. Even though businesses are cautious about future spending plans such as capital expenditures, actual demand for business loans is growing. Commercial and industrial loan growth, which is the pace at which banks lend to businesses for operations, equipment or expansion, is roughly 4% year-over-year. This willingness to plan for the future aligns with the reduction in economic policy uncertainty, now around half of its April peak, and provides a more stable environment for corporate planning. While tariff impacts continue to build, potentially leading to a Federal Reserve rate cut, there are opportunities ahead.

node.text:
In Naples, Richard Grows is building a team equipped to serve clients through every stage of their financial lives, with advice tailored to their unique goals, and a relationship they can rely on today and for generations to come. With deep roots in Florida, we’re proud to expand alongside the community we serve. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how our Naples team can help you.

node.text: The US Open Tennis Championships gathers the world’s best players on one of the sport’s most iconic stages. As proud sponsors for the last 44 years, we are always amazed by the level of athleticism, skill and sportsmanship shown by each of the competitors. Wishing the best of luck to all players competing in the final Grand Slam of 2025. #usopen #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
With healthcare trading at steep discounts, Michael Cembalest, our Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, examines the sector’s relative valuation in his latest Eye on the Market. Explore the factors dragging healthcare to its lowest valuations in over 30 years and potential catalysts for a rebound, along with the latest in the battle over publicly funded scientific research in the United States.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
Many eagerly awaited the July Consumer Price Index (CPI) data for signs of tariff impacts on the economy. This week’s chart highlights these insights, breaking down core inflation (excluding food and energy), goods and services. Year-over-year core CPI rose nearly 30 basis points since May—the highest jump in six months—partly driven by higher tariff costs passed to consumers. Despite this, the market welcomed the report, as the rise in core goods was less severe than anticipated.

We see nothing in the data that would make the Federal Reserve more responsive to higher inflation than the recent softening in the labor market. We still expect 50 basis points of Federal Reserve rate cuts by year-end.

J.P. Morgan Private Bank made a distinguished appearance in Aspen this year, hosting an exceptional event at the rooftop of the Aspen Art Museum. Our program featured a conversation with entrepreneur, founder, and innovator Mark Cuban and David Frame, CEO of the Global Private Bank, who discussed advancements in healthcare, sports investing, the evolving landscape of technology and navigating generational wealth. 

Our presence in Aspen highlights our unwavering commitment to delivering thought-provoking content directly to our clients across the United States.

Explore how J.P. Morgan Private Bank can assist you in navigating these promising opportunities.

Recent labor market data reflects a broader trend of cooling momentum in the U.S. economy. Last Friday, the job market data came in below expectations, but what caught investors’ attention were the revisions from May and June, showing 258,000 fewer jobs than initially reported. We expect real GDP growth to slow to around 1% by year-end, with unemployment gradually rising in the latter half of the year as real income and consumer spending soften. Despite near-term pressures from tariffs that may weigh on activity, the capex of tech giants has persisted through volatility, and we see continuous opportunities as the AI ecosystem continues to expand. In the current market environment, portfolio resilience and diversification are key, with AI continuing to offer a compelling long-term investment opportunity.

node.text:
Rooted in the Salt Lake City community, Danielle Wright leads with a deep commitment to strengthening the place she and her family call home. From business owners to multigenerational families, she and her team are dedicated to helping clients across Utah and Idaho grow, preserve and manage their wealth with strategies tailored to their unique goals. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how our Salt Lake City team can help you.

The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) is meeting today to decide if it’s the right time for rate cuts, as investors eagerly await the Federal Reserve’s next steps. Despite resilient labor market data and steady inflation, the Fed has kept its benchmark interest rate on hold amid tariff-related economic uncertainty. The risk, as seen by the Fed? Tariff uncertainty drags on, potentially de-anchoring inflation expectations—which is why they’ve been in a wait-and-see mode. However, the Fed has acknowledged that while they expect prices to rise due to tariffs, this should be a one-off increase with inflation expectations remaining well anchored.

Looking ahead, the market anticipates the Fed will resume the rate-cutting cycle in September, especially as clarity emerges with two more inflation and employment reports over the next few months. At 2 p.m. today, Fed officials will release a post-meeting statement, offering further insights. We will continue to monitor the situation, but our view remains that there will be 50 basis points of cuts by year-end and another 50 basis points by the middle of next year.

node.text:
AI investments are surging across government and private sectors, driving transformative changes in the United States. Private companies are rapidly raising capital and leading the charge, riding the AI wave to expand and innovate. The latest development is the Trump administration’s recent $70 billion investment in AI and energy, which underscores growing support for these technologies.

This influx of capital is accelerating AI adoption, with the share of U.S. firms using AI doubling in the past year, and adoption rates growing at 4.5x the pace seen in 2024. While adoption is most significant in Information and Professional Services, it’s evident across all sectors, showcasing AI’s scalable potential. Embracing AI as a key theme offers access to high-growth opportunities.

node.text:
We had an extraordinary experience returning to the beautiful Norrviken Gardens in Båstad, Sweden, to celebrate the best of luxury Nordic lifestyle.
 
@theauroraconcours united timeless elegance with automotive excellence. Guests enjoyed black-tie galas and cocktail receptions, and our J.P. Morgan Private Bank Lounge provided a refined space for us to connect over our deeply held passion for innovation and craftsmanship.
 
We were proud to collaborate with names such Koenigsegg and Motikon to support this unique occasion for a second year.
 
Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

Did you know that we’ve published 584 editions of Eye on the Market since 2005? For two decades, Michael Cembalest, Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, has shared his wisdom with clients on markets, investments, politics and more. For the special 20th anniversary retrospective edition, we’re sharing 30 of his most memorable insights from the archives that are just as relevant for the future as they were for the past. 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text: Tariffs have returned to the spotlight amid a recent wave of headlines. Meanwhile, U.S. tariff revenues have surged, with June collections tripling those of the first quarter as the current implied effective tariff rate has increased from approximately 2.5% to around 8%–9%. While recent announcements suggest a potential rise to about 18% in the coming weeks, more than double the current rate, we believe some of these threats may not stick. Our base case remains that effective tariff rates will settle between 10% and 15%. Consequently, we foresee a modest drag on growth. As negotiations progress, whether rates hold or not, investors will at least gain clarity.

node.text:
For Market Team Lead Danielle Wright, serving clients in Utah and Idaho goes hand in hand with serving the community. She and her team are proud to contribute meaningfully through local organizations, and they bring the same level of commitment to supporting the individuals and families in this region in achieving their unique goals. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Salt Lake City team.

node.text:
We’re excited to showcase SailGP as part of our annual J.P. Morgan NextList—a curated selection of books, experiences and innovations spotlighting emerging cultural trends. 

This high-adrenaline global league is redefining sailing with its unique, equalized approach to competition. National teams face off in identical F50 foiling catamarans, meaning that victory hinges on the talent and teamwork of participating crews, not technological advantages.

Tap the link in bio to explore the full list and learn more.
